sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The click on the first green block worked! Looking at the image, the first green block (top-left) has changed from green to light blue (sky blue). This is different from before - previously clicking on green blocks removed them entirely. Now clicking changes their color.
Let me check the current state to understand what changed.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check what changed after clicking on the first green block
seg = current_frame.segmentation

# Check all nodes
print(f"Total nodes: {len(seg['nodes'])}")

# Check for green blocks
green_blocks = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'N']
print(f"Green blocks: {len(green_blocks)}")

# Check for sky blue blocks
sky_blue = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'S']
print(f"Sky blue blocks: {len(sky_blue)}")
for n in sky_blue:
    b = n['boundary']
    min_r = min(p[0] for p in b)
    min_c = min(p[1] for p in b)
    print(f"  Sky blue: ({min_r},{min_c}), {n['pixels']} px")

# Check for white blocks
white_blocks = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'W']
print(f"White blocks: {len(white_blocks)}")
for n in white_blocks:
    b = n['boundary']
    min_r = min(p[0] for p in b)
    min_c = min(p[1] for p in b)
    print(f"  White: ({min_r},{min_c}), {n['pixels']} px")

# Check last action result
print(f"\nLast action result: {last_action_result}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
